notableMoments: Hosted the 2010 World Cup, becoming the first African nation to stage the tournament, an event that united the nation and captivated the world
Siphiwe Tshabalala scored the opening goal of the 2010 World Cup against Mexico, one of the most iconic moments in South African sporting history
Known as Bafana Bafana ('The Boys'), the team has been a symbol of post-apartheid South African pride and unity since readmission to international football in 1992
First World Cup appearance in 1998 in France, just four years after the end of apartheid, was a powerful symbol of South Africa's return to the global stage
Despite hosting the 2010 World Cup, South Africa became the first host nation in tournament history to be eliminated in the group stage
Won the 1996 Africa Cup of Nations on home soil, a triumph that galvanized the newly democratic nation and demonstrated football's power to unite
The vuvuzela, made famous worldwide during the 2010 World Cup, became one of the most recognizable symbols of South African football culture
Qualifying for the 2026 World Cup marks Bafana Bafana's return to the tournament for the first time since hosting in 2010, ending a 16-year absence
